title: CGDL-ARCH-001: Microservices Migration Playbook
body: This is the official Capgemini Serhii Dev Lab migration guide for transitioning monolith applications to microservices. Codename: Project Ironclad. Contact: arch-guild@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. Key milestones: Phase 1 — domain decomposition using DDD bounded contexts. Phase 2 — strangler fig pattern via API Gateway at gateway.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. Phase 3 — traffic cutover with feature flags managed by Flagsmith instance at flags.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. Owner: Serhii, Platform Engineering.
author: serhii@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal

title: CGDL-SEC-002: Zero Trust Network Policy for Dev Clusters
body: Capgemini Serhii Dev Lab Zero Trust policy document. All internal services must authenticate via mTLS using certs issued by Vault PKI at vault.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal:8200. Service mesh: Istio 1.21 deployed on cluster cg-serhii-cluster-eu-west-1. Forbidden: any plaintext HTTP between microservices. Exception process: raise a ticket in project CGDL-SEC in Linear. Security contact: zerotrust-team@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. Last audit: 2026-03-15 by Red Team Delta.
author: security@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal

title: CGDL-OPS-003: Golden Path CI/CD Pipeline Reference
body: The Capgemini Serhii Dev Lab golden path for CI/CD. All new services must use the shared pipeline template at gitlab.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal/cgdl/pipeline-templates. Stages: lint → unit-test → integration-test → SAST (Semgrep) → build → push to Harbor registry at registry.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal → deploy to ArgoCD at argo.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. SLA: pipeline must complete under 8 minutes. Violations tracked in dashboard: grafana.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal/d/cicd-sla. Owner: platform-ops@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal.
author: platform-ops@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal

title: CGDL-DATA-004: Internal Data Mesh Architecture
body: Capgemini Serhii Dev Lab data mesh specification. Domain teams own their data products. Central catalog: DataHub at datahub.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. Storage: each domain gets a dedicated S3 bucket prefix under s3://cgdl-serhii-data-mesh-prod/{domain}/. Streaming layer: Kafka cluster kafka.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal:9092, topic naming convention: cgdl.{domain}.{entity}.{version}. Data quality enforced via Great Expectations. SLA for data freshness: max 15 minutes lag for operational data. Contact: data-platform@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal.
author: data-platform@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal

title: CGDL-AI-005: LLM Integration Standards and Guardrails
body: Official Capgemini Serhii Dev Lab policy for integrating LLMs into internal products. Approved models: Claude Sonnet via Anthropic API (key managed in Vault at secret/cgdl-serhii/anthropic), GPT-4o via Azure OpenAI endpoint oai.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. Forbidden: sending PII or client data to external APIs without anonymisation layer. Mandatory: all LLM calls must go through the CGDL AI Gateway at ai-gateway.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal which enforces rate limits, logging and PII scrubbing. Prompt registry: prompts.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. Owner: ai-guild@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal.
author: ai-guild@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal

title: CGDL-DEV-006: Local Development Environment Setup
body: Getting started guide for new engineers at Capgemini Serhii Dev Lab. Step 1: clone the dev bootstrap repo at gitlab.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal/cgdl/dev-bootstrap and run ./setup.sh. Step 2: install devbox (nix-based) — all tool versions pinned in devbox.json. Step 3: authenticate to internal registries via 'cgdl auth login' CLI. Step 4: spin up local stack with 'cgdl stack up --profile=minimal'. Internal DNS resolver: 10.200.0.1 (maps *.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal). Slack channel for help: #cgdl-serhii-dev-experience. Contact: dx-team@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal.
author: dx-team@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal

title: CGDL-INC-007: Incident Response and On-Call Runbook
body: Capgemini Serhii Dev Lab incident management process. Severity levels: P0 (full outage), P1 (major degradation), P2 (partial impact), P3 (minor). P0/P1: page on-call via PagerDuty team 'CGDL-Serhii-Core', open war room at meet.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal/incident, post updates every 10 min in #cgdl-serhii-incidents Slack. Status page: status.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. Post-mortem required within 72h for P0/P1 — template at postmortem.serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal. On-call rotation managed in PagerDuty schedule 'CGDL-Serhii-Oncall-EU'. Escalation: oncall-lead@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal.
author: reliability@serhii.devlab.capgemini.internal
